headline: Endau-Rompin Protected Area Budget Cut 42% Since 2020
context: JNP data shows Endau-Rompin National Park's management budget was cut from RM8.4M in 2020 to RM4.9M in 2025, coinciding with a 3x increase in illegal encroachment incidents.

cards.big: Endau-Rompin spans 80,000 hectares across Johor and Pahang.
cards.sub: Its management budget was cut 42% in 5 years. Illegal encroachment tripled in the same period.

cards.big: 17 ranger positions are vacant out of a sanctioned strength of 45.
cards.sub: Starting salary for a park ranger is RM1,500. Private security guards earn RM1,800.

cards.big: Sumatran rhinoceros was last recorded in Endau-Rompin in 2012.
cards.sub: The species is functionally extinct in Peninsular Malaysia. Under-protection made extinction inevitable.

cards.big: Endau-Rompin generates RM12M annually in ecosystem services.
cards.sub: Water catchment, flood protection, and carbon storage — none of which appear in the park's budget justification.

cards.big: Cutting a park budget while its ecosystem services are worth RM12M is paying less to protect more.
National parks need funding models that capture their economic value. Entry fees alone cannot sustain 80,000 hectares. Payment for ecosystem services offers a viable alternative.
